title: Cracca secunda
uses: Cracca secunda, also known as Stickseed, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Stickseed has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Its roots and leaves are often used in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and constipation. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro:
I couldn't find information on the plant "Cracca secunda." However, I found that Cracca is an alternative spelling for Craccaea or Craccaea secunda, which is a synonym for Psychotria secunda or Psychotria viridis.
